message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes.

FINDING REL-1:
The SIGTERM handler (observability/tracing.py) handled prior callable and prior==SIG_DFL but NOT prior is None (signal.getsignal returns None for a C-installed prior handler), so it flushed and returned → swallowed SIGTERM. Fix: 'elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:' → reset to SIG_DFL and os.kill(getpid, signum) to re-deliver. VERIFY: with getsignal monkeypatched to None, the installed handler re-establishes SIG_DFL and re-delivers (os.kill called). Test: test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_when_prior_is_none_c_handler. Confirm SIG_IGN still stays ignored and the callable-prior path still chains.

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest"; gh for CI). Judge ONLY REL-1. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
./src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py
---git show files---
commit d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 13:34:33 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE. Two are fix-regressions of pass-9's own fixes.
    
    - SEC-10-1 (high): libpq PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR/PGSERVICE env vars override/supply the host even
      when the DSN looks loopback (PGHOSTADDR wins over host=localhost) → graph state off-box
      despite require_local_uri passing. Fix: _assert_local_libpq_env() validates PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR
      (loopback/socket, comma-split) and rejects PGSERVICE/PGSERVICEFILE (a service file can
      redirect off-box, unvalidatable inline), called from get_postgres_connection_string.
    - P10-PII-1 (high): the operator's real LAN topology (gateway [REDACTED], host
      [REDACTED], interface) leaked into committed transcripts from captured route/lsof
      output — no IP rule. Fix: RFC1918 redaction (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with digit/dot
      lookarounds (catches the \n-glued form, never matches inside a longer number); loopback
      127.x, 0.0.0.0 and public IPs preserved. Re-scrubbed corpus (literal private IPs now 0).
    - P10-CI-1 (high, fix-reg of CI-1): require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest run's
      conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run certified a newer untested HEAD. Fix:
      fetch headSha and require it == git rev-parse origin/<branch>; fail closed if no completed
      run for the current commit.
    - P10-MO-1 (high): a set/frozenset payload is hashable but does NOT round-trip
      (unfreeze→list→re-freeze→tuple) so verify_records SILENTLY failed on a rebuilt chain. Fix:
      ProvenanceEntry rejects set/frozenset payload leaves loudly at record time (use a sorted
      list/tuple, which round-trips and digests identically).
    - P10-MO-2 (med): from_records raised a raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field.
      Fix: ProvenanceEntry raises typed ProvenanceError when payload is not a Mapping.
    - P10-PII-2 (low, fix-reg of P9-PII-1): the machine-hostname rule lacked a leading \b →
      substring over-redaction (host 'host' redacted inside 'localhost'). Fix: leading \b.
    - REL-1 (low, fix-reg of pass-8 K-REL-1): SIGTERM handler swallowed the signal when
      getsignal() returns None (prior handler installed from C). Fix: treat None like SIG_DFL and
      re-deliver the default terminate.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 575 passed,
    5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

 scripts/capture_transcripts.py                     |  18 +-

content.text: REL-1 is already committed in HEAD. Let me view the SIGTERM handler and find the diff plus the test.

content.content:
1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	
31	
32	def _int_env(name: str, default: str) -> int:
33	    """Parse an int env var with a clear error naming the var (#CV-S-1, same class as #H-022).
34	
35	    A bare ``int(os.environ[...])`` at import time raises an opaque "invalid literal for int()"
36	    that names neither the var nor fires inside any handler.
37	    """
38	    raw = os.environ.get(name, default)
39	    try:
40	        return int(raw)
41	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
42	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an integer; got {raw!r}") from exc
43	
44	
45	TRACING_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006")
46	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
47	
48	_provider: Any = None
49	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
50	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
51	
52	
53	def _disabled() -> bool:
54	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
55	
56	
57	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
58	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
59	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
60	
61	
62	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
63	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
64	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
65	
66	
67	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
68	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
69	
70	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
71	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
72	    """
73	    global _provider
74	    if _disabled():
75	        return None
76	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
77	    with _provider_lock:
78	        if _provider is not None:
79	            return _provider
80	        try:
81	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
82	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
83	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
84	            from phoenix.otel import register
85	
86	            provider = register(
87	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
88	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
89	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
90	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
91	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
92	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
93	            )
94	            _provider = provider
95	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
96	            _instrument_langchain()
97	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
98	            return _provider
99	        except Exception as exc:
100	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
101	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
102	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
103	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
104	            _log.warning(
105	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
106	                type(exc).__name__,
107	                exc,
108	            )
109	            return None
110	
111	
112	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
113	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
114	    try:
115	        from opentelemetry import trace
116	
117	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
118	            _log.warning(
119	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
120	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
121	            )
122	    except Exception:
123	        pass
124	
125	
126	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
127	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
128	    try:
129	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
130	
131	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
132	    except Exception:
133	        pass
134	
135	
136	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
137	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
138	
139	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
140	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
141	    """
142	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
143	    if not callable(flush):
144	        return
145	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
146	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
147	        try:
148	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
149	
150	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
151	                _safe(flush)
152	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
153	                # Preserve the ORIGINAL disposition (#K-REL-1). If a Python handler pre-existed,
154	                # chain to it. Otherwise prior is SIG_DFL/SIG_IGN — both NON-callable — and simply
155	                # returning here would SWALLOW the signal: we replaced SIGTERM's default
156	                # terminate-the-process action with a flush-and-return, so `docker stop` / `kill` /
157	                # k8s would hang until SIGKILL escalation. Re-establish and re-deliver the default
158	                # action so the process still dies on SIGTERM (SIG_IGN stays ignored).
159	                if callable(prior):
160	                    prior(signum, frame)
161	                elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:
162	                    # prior is None when the previous SIGTERM handler was installed from NON-Python
163	                    # (C) code (signal.getsignal returns None for "unknown handler") — we cannot
164	                    # chain to it, so treat it like SIG_DFL and re-deliver the default terminate
165	                    # rather than swallow the signal (#REL-1, the K-REL-1 class for a C prior).
166	                    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIG_DFL)
167	                    os.kill(os.getpid(), signum)  # re-deliver → default terminate action runs
168	
169	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
170	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
171	            pass
172	
173	
174	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
175	    try:
176	        fn()
177	    except Exception:
178	        pass
179	
180	
181	@contextmanager
182	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
183	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
184	
185	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
186	    """
187	    if run_id is None:
188	        yield
189	        return
190	    try:
191	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
192	    except ImportError:
193	        yield
194	        return
195	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
196	    try:
197	        yield
198	    finally:
199	        context.detach(token)
200	
201	
202	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
203	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
204	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
205	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
206	
207	
208	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
209	class Tracer(Protocol):
210	    @contextmanager
211	    def span(
212	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
213	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
214	
215	
216	class OtelTracer:
217	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
218	
219	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
220	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
221	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
222	    """
223	
224	    @contextmanager
225	    def span(
226	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
227	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
228	        try:
229	            from opentelemetry import trace
230	        except ImportError:
231	            yield
232	            return
233	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
234	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
235	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
236	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
237	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
238	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
239	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
240	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
241	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
242	            yield
243	
244	
245	class UnconfiguredTracer:
246	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
247	
248	    @contextmanager
249	    def span(
250	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
251	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
252	        raise NotImplementedError(
253	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
254	            "test double"
255	        )
256	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
257

commit d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 13:34:33 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE. Two are fix-regressions of pass-9's own fixes.
    
    - SEC-10-1 (high): libpq PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR/PGSERVICE env vars override/supply the host even
      when the DSN looks loopback (PGHOSTADDR wins over host=localhost) → graph state off-box
      despite require_local_uri passing. Fix: _assert_local_libpq_env() validates PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR
      (loopback/socket, comma-split) and rejects PGSERVICE/PGSERVICEFILE (a service file can
      redirect off-box, unvalidatable inline), called from get_postgres_connection_string.
    - P10-PII-1 (high): the operator's real LAN topology (gateway [REDACTED], host
      [REDACTED], interface) leaked into committed transcripts from captured route/lsof
      output — no IP rule. Fix: RFC1918 redaction (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with digit/dot
      lookarounds (catches the \n-glued form, never matches inside a longer number); loopback
      127.x, 0.0.0.0 and public IPs preserved. Re-scrubbed corpus (literal private IPs now 0).
    - P10-CI-1 (high, fix-reg of CI-1): require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest run's
      conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run certified a newer untested HEAD. Fix:
      fetch headSha and require it == git rev-parse origin/<branch>; fail closed if no completed
      run for the current commit.
    - P10-MO-1 (high): a set/frozenset payload is hashable but does NOT round-trip
      (unfreeze→list→re-freeze→tuple) so verify_records SILENTLY failed on a rebuilt chain. Fix:
      ProvenanceEntry rejects set/frozenset payload leaves loudly at record time (use a sorted
      list/tuple, which round-trips and digests identically).
    - P10-MO-2 (med): from_records raised a raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field.
      Fix: ProvenanceEntry raises typed ProvenanceError when payload is not a Mapping.
    - P10-PII-2 (low, fix-reg of P9-PII-1): the machine-hostname rule lacked a leading \b →
      substring over-redaction (host 'host' redacted inside 'localhost'). Fix: leading \b.
    - REL-1 (low, fix-reg of pass-8 K-REL-1): SIGTERM handler swallowed the signal when
      getsignal() returns None (prior handler installed from C). Fix: treat None like SIG_DFL and
      re-deliver the default terminate.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 575 passed,
    5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py
index ae898ad..ec7b101 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py
@@ -158,7 +158,11 @@ def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
                 # action so the process still dies on SIGTERM (SIG_IGN stays ignored).
                 if callable(prior):
                     prior(signum, frame)
-                elif prior == signal.SIG_DFL:
+                elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:
+                    # prior is None when the previous SIGTERM handler was installed from NON-Python
+                    # (C) code (signal.getsignal returns None for "unknown handler") — we cannot
+                    # chain to it, so treat it like SIG_DFL and re-deliver the default terminate
+                    # rather than swallow the signal (#REL-1, the K-REL-1 class for a C prior).
                     signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIG_DFL)
                     os.kill(os.getpid(), signum)  # re-deliver → default terminate action runs

content.text: The fix matches the description. Now let me find and examine the regression test.

content.content:
tests/test_observability.py:199:def test_install_flush_on_exit_wires_atexit_and_sigterm(monkeypatch):
tests/test_observability.py:226:    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
tests/test_observability.py:244:def test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_default_termination(monkeypatch):
tests/test_observability.py:278:    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
tests/test_observability.py:289:def test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_when_prior_is_none_c_handler(monkeypatch):
tests/test_observability.py:323:    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
tests/test_observability.py:331:def test_install_flush_on_exit_noop_without_force_flush(monkeypatch):
tests/test_observability.py:335:    tr._install_flush_on_exit(object())

199	def test_install_flush_on_exit_wires_atexit_and_sigterm(monkeypatch):
200	    # span-flush on exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM must be wired and call force_flush/shutdown,
201	    # else buffered provenance spans are lost on container stop. No backend needed.
202	    import signal as _signal
203	
204	    calls = {"flush": 0, "shutdown": 0, "atexit": []}
205	
206	    class _Provider:
207	        def force_flush(self):
208	            calls["flush"] += 1
209	
210	        def shutdown(self):
211	            calls["shutdown"] += 1
212	
213	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: calls["atexit"].append(fn))
214	    captured = {}
215	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "getsignal", lambda s: _signal.SIG_DFL)
216	
217	    def _capture_signal(sig, handler):
218	        if handler is not _signal.SIG_DFL:  # the install call (not the #K-REL-1 reset-to-default)
219	            captured["h"] = handler
220	
221	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "signal", _capture_signal)
222	    # The handler now RE-DELIVERS SIGTERM when prior is SIG_DFL (#K-REL-1); stub os.kill so invoking
223	    # it here doesn't actually signal the test process.
224	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.os, "kill", lambda pid, sig: None)
225	
226	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
227	
228	    import threading
229	
230	    assert calls["atexit"], "atexit flush handler not registered"
231	    calls["atexit"][0]()  # simulate interpreter exit
232	    assert calls["flush"] >= 1
233	    # #G-014: the SIGTERM assertion was gated on `if "h" in captured`, so removing the SIGTERM
234	    # wiring silently skipped it. pytest runs on the main thread, where the handler MUST be
235	    # installed — assert it unconditionally there so a regression that drops the wiring fails.
236	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
237	        assert "h" in captured, "SIGTERM flush handler not installed on the main thread (#G-014)"
238	        captured["h"](_signal.SIGTERM, None)
239	        assert calls["shutdown"] >= 1
240	    else:  # pragma: no cover - the suite runs on the main thread
241	        pass
242	
243	
244	def test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_default_termination(monkeypatch):
245	    # #K-REL-1: when no prior Python SIGTERM handler exists, getsignal returns SIG_DFL (NOT
246	    # callable). The handler must NOT just flush-and-return — that would SWALLOW SIGTERM (it
247	    # replaced the default terminate action), so docker stop / kill / k8s would hang until SIGKILL.
248	    # It must re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so the process still terminates.
249	    import signal as _signal
250	
251	    calls = {"flush": 0, "shutdown": 0, "reset": [], "killed": []}
252	
253	    class _Provider:
254	        def force_flush(self):
255	            calls["flush"] += 1
256	
257	        def shutdown(self):
258	            calls["shutdown"] += 1
259	
260	    captured = {}
261	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: None)
262	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "getsignal", lambda s: _signal.SIG_DFL)
263	
264	    def _fake_signal(sig, handler):
265	        if handler is _signal.SIG_DFL:
266	            calls["reset"].append(sig)  # the re-establish-default call
267	        else:
268	            captured["h"] = handler  # the install call
269	
270	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "signal", _fake_signal)
271	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.os, "kill", lambda pid, sig: calls["killed"].append((pid, sig)))
272	
273	    import threading
274	
275	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():  # pragma: no cover
276	        pytest.skip("SIGTERM wiring only on the main thread")
277	
278	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
279	    assert "h" in captured
280	    captured["h"](_signal.SIGTERM, None)
281	    # flushed AND shut down AND re-delivered the default terminate (reset to SIG_DFL + os.kill)
282	    assert calls["flush"] >= 1 and calls["shutdown"] >= 1
283	    assert _signal.SIGTERM in calls["reset"], "did not re-establish default SIGTERM disposition"
284	    assert calls["killed"] and calls["killed"][0][1] == _signal.SIGTERM, (
285	        "did not re-deliver SIGTERM — termination is swallowed (#K-REL-1)"
286	    )
287	
288	
289	def test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_when_prior_is_none_c_handler(monkeypatch):
290	    # #REL-1: signal.getsignal() returns None when the previous SIGTERM handler was installed from
291	    # NON-Python (C) code. None is neither callable nor == SIG_DFL, so the pass-8 fix's branches
292	    # both miss it and the handler would flush-and-return → swallow SIGTERM again. It must treat
293	    # None like SIG_DFL and re-deliver the default terminate.
294	    import signal as _signal
295	
296	    calls = {"reset": [], "killed": []}
297	
298	    class _Provider:
299	        def force_flush(self):
300	            pass
301	
302	        def shutdown(self):
303	            pass
304	
305	    captured = {}
306	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: None)
307	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "getsignal", lambda s: None)  # C-installed prior handler
308	
309	    def _fake_signal(sig, handler):
310	        if handler is _signal.SIG_DFL:
311	            calls["reset"].append(sig)
312	        else:
313	            captured["h"] = handler
314	
315	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "signal", _fake_signal)
316	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.os, "kill", lambda pid, sig: calls["killed"].append((pid, sig)))
317	
318	    import threading
319	
320	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():  # pragma: no cover
321	        pytest.skip("SIGTERM wiring only on the main thread")
322	
323	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
324	    assert "h" in captured
325	    captured["h"](_signal.SIGTERM, None)
326	    assert _signal.SIGTERM in calls["reset"] and calls["killed"], (
327	        "prior=None (C handler) swallowed SIGTERM instead of re-delivering (#REL-1)"
328	    )
329	
330	
331	def test_install_flush_on_exit_noop_without_force_flush(monkeypatch):
332	    # a provider lacking force_flush must not register anything (no crash)
333	    calls = []
334	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: calls.append(fn))
335	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(object())
336	    assert calls == []

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'EOF'
import signal, os
# Simulate the handler branch logic for each prior value
class P:
    def force_flush(self): pass
    def shutdown(self): pass

for prior_name, prior in [("SIG_IGN", signal.SIG_IGN), ("None(C)", None), ("SIG_DFL", signal.SIG_DFL), ("callable", lambda s,f: None)]:
    reset=[]; killed=[]; chained=[]
    def emulate(prior=prior):
        # mirror _handler branch decisions
        if callable(prior):
            chained.append(True); return "chain"
        elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:
            reset.append(True); killed.append(True); return "redeliver"
        else:
            return "return(ignore-preserved)"
    print(f"{prior_name:10} -> {emulate()}  reset={bool(reset)} killed={bool(killed)} chained={bool(chained)}")
EOF
echo "=== git status (must be clean, only untracked PASS-10.md) ==="
git status --short
echo "=== full observability suite ==="
python -m pytest -q tests/test_observability.py 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
The `tracing.py` matches HEAD exactly (no diff). The `capture_transcripts.py` change is a pre-existing working-tree modification unrelated to REL-1 (different finding, P10-PII-1 area) — not something I introduced and outside my scope to judge.

My review of REL-1 is complete. Summary of findings:

**Appropriate (root cause correct):** `signal.getsignal()` returns `None` when the prior SIGTERM handler was installed from C code. The pass-8 fix only handled `callable(prior)` (chain) and `prior == signal.SIG_DFL` (re-deliver); `None` fell through both branches, so the handler flushed and returned, swallowing SIGTERM — exactly the K-REL-1 class it was meant to fix. The fix `elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:` is the right layer (the same branch) and the right approach: re-establish SIG_DFL + `os.kill(getpid, signum)` to re-deliver the default terminate. Correct, because a C-installed prior handler cannot be invoked from Python anyway, so treating it like SIG_DFL is the only safe choice (preferring termination over swallowing).

**Complete (no residual gap):**
- SIG_IGN still stays ignored: it's not callable, not None, not == SIG_DFL → falls through all branches → handler returns without reset/kill, preserving "ignore" semantics. Verified by branch-logic emulation.
- The callable-prior path still chains. Verified.
- SIG_DFL path unchanged. Verified.

**Test discriminates:** `tests/test_observability.py::test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_when_prior_is_none_c_handler` monkeypatches `getsignal` to return `None`, invokes the installed handler, and asserts both reset-to-SIG_DFL and `os.kill` re-delivery. Reverting the one-line fix causes ONLY this test to FAIL (`SIGTERM in calls["reset"]` empty), while the SIG_DFL test still passes — proving the test isolates the None/C-handler case. With the fix, all 4 SIGTERM/flush tests pass; full observability suite 27 passed / 2 skipped.

Relevant files:
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py` (lines 159-167)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_observability.py` (lines 289-328)

One minor note (not a REL-1 defect): the fix has no dedicated explicit test asserting SIG_IGN stays ignored — it's covered only by logical fall-through, not a regression test. The behavior is correct, so this is a coverage nit, not a gap.

input.rationale: REL-1 fix is both appropriate and complete. Root cause: signal.getsignal() returns None for a C-installed prior SIGTERM handler; the pass-8 fix only branched on callable(prior) and prior==SIG_DFL, so None fell through both and the handler flushed-and-returned, swallowing SIGTERM (the K-REL-1 class). The fix 'elif prior is None or prior == signal.SIG_DFL:' is at the correct layer and uses the correct approach (re-establish SIG_DFL via signal.signal + re-deliver via os.kill(getpid, signum)); treating a non-chainable C prior like SIG_DFL and preferring termination over swallow is the only safe choice. Verified all four prior dispositions: callable->chain, None(C)->reset+redeliver, SIG_DFL->reset+redeliver, SIG_IGN->return (ignore preserved, since it is not callable/None/SIG_DFL). The regression test test_sigterm_handler_redelivers_when_prior_is_none_c_handler DISCRIMINATES: monkeypatching getsignal to None, reverting only the 'prior is None or' clause makes exactly this test FAIL on the swallowed-SIGTERM assertion (reset empty, killed empty) while the SIG_DFL test still passes; restoring the fix makes all 4 pass. Full observability suite: 27 passed, 2 skipped. tracing.py matches HEAD exactly.
concerns: Minor coverage nit (not a REL-1 defect): no explicit regression test asserts SIG_IGN stays ignored; that path is verified only by logical fall-through, not a discriminating test. Behavior is correct.
Unrelated: the working tree shows a pre-existing modification to scripts/capture_transcripts.py (different finding's area), outside REL-1 scope and not introduced by this review.
